purpose: The commit fixes a bug where commands were being queued even after the HCI_UNREGISTER flag was set, which could lead to a use-after-free (UAF) error. It modifies hci_cmd_sync_queue to return an error in such cases.
The commit removes an unnecessary include statement in n_hdlc.c. The author explains that asm/termios.h is not needed because another header already includes it, simplifying the code.
The commit updates documentation to reflect changes in the report table for the 'N/A' metric added for store operations.
The commit addresses issues in vma lock handling during splitting and unmapping in hugetlb. It fixes race conditions and ensures proper allocation of locks for all VM_MAYSHARE vmas.

The commit adds documentation for AVDD and MICVDD supplies in the dt-bindings for the realtek,rt5682s codec. These supplies are already used elsewhere, so this commit ensures they are properly documented.
The commit fixes a resource leak in the zorro7xx SCSI driver's remove function by adding an iounmap() call that was previously missing.
The commit removes an unused work field from the struct mlx5e_async_ctx, which improves code quality by eliminating unnecessary code.

The commit changes the type of cfg80211_auth_request::key_idx from unsigned to signed to prevent warnings when assigning -1, which indicates a NULL key. This avoids issues with static analysis tools like smatch.
The commit sorts the CONFIG_SURFACE_PLATFORMS configuration in RISC-V defconfigs without introducing any functional changes. It aligns the configuration order with how savedefconfig operates.

The commit introduces a helper function mcp251xfd_is_fd_mode() to replace an open-coded check in the CAN driver for MCP251xfd chips. This improves code readability and maintainability by abstracting the check into a dedicated function.
The commit modifies selftests related to the RTC subsystem to skip tests when the RTC hardware is not present. This prevents unnecessary test failures in environments without RTC support.
The commit removes an unnecessary variable assignment that was causing a compiler warning. It improves code quality by eliminating dead code.

The commit modifies the kernel configuration to allow rcuscale without requiring RCU Tasks. This change prevents unnecessary building of RCU Tasks when rcuscale is enabled, reducing kernel size and complexity.
The commit removes duplicated text in spte.h, improving code readability.

The commit reverts a previous change that populated the dcn315 clk table based on dcfclk. This was causing issues due to how pmfw fills out the table when certain states are disabled, leading to a 'no read situation' and underflow. The revert is done to prevent these issues until a better solution can be implemented.
The commit adds a compatible string for the Renesas RZ/V2L SoC's Mali-G31 GPU in device tree bindings, enabling proper hardware recognition.
The commit renames struct pcie_port to dw_pcie_rp for consistency with other naming conventions in the DW PCIe core driver.
The commit fixes a clang -Wformat warning by correcting the format specifier in debug logging. The argument was being promoted to int, so using %x instead of %hx ensures correct formatting.
The commit fixes a bug in the MIPS jump_label code by casting the upper bound of a branch range to a long for signed comparison, preventing negative offsets from triggering warnings.

The commit removes a specific case from the SetHwReg8188EU() function that was redundantly calling another function. This change simplifies the code and is part of an effort to eliminate unnecessary layers.
The commit addresses sparse warnings by declaring non-global symbols as static in the arm64 kernel code.
The commit removes incorrect interrupt flags from MDSS nodes in device tree bindings, fixing a bug where consumers were using these flags improperly.
The commit modifies the allocation of arrays within struct ctrl_ref to prepare for future driver changes in array size.
The commit adds support for new SoCs (SM8350, SC8280XP, SA8540P, SM8450) and various PMICs to the socinfo driver, expanding hardware compatibility.
The commit updates the wifi driver rtl8xxxu to recognize all possible chip versions (A-P) instead of showing 'unknown' for versions beyond E. This improves handling of different chip cuts.
This commit removes redundant state management for PRCMU clocks in the ux500 platform by dropping a custom software enable count since the core already handles it.
The commit aims to refactor RISC-V mm initialization by reducing #ifdef CONFIG_XIP_KERNEL usage through grouping similar cases. It improves code structure but doesn't fix bugs or add features.
The commit enables QUP and GPI DMA hardware in the device tree for the sm6350-lena platform. This change is preparatory work to allow adding functioning I2C nodes in the future.
The commit improves comment clarity by renaming variables, fixing typos, and adjusting comment formatting to adhere to coding standards.

The commit fixes an infinite loop that occurs when releasing resources if the HW semaphore cannot be acquired, typically due to hardware being removed unexpectedly. The fix involves logging an error and exiting instead of looping indefinitely.
The commit updates the pcm3168a driver to use modern ASoC DAI format terminology, replacing old-style defines with current clocking terms.
The commit removes unnecessary code related to checking if a file is a swapfile in NFS, as this check is no longer needed because IS_SWAPFILE is always defined.

The commit implements handling of GTP Echo Request messages by sending appropriate Echo Responses, including specific information elements based on GTP version. This ensures compliance with the GTP specification.

The commit replaces the use of cpumask_next_* functions with cpumask_first_* where appropriate. It introduces a new function cpumask_first_zero() to handle cases where n == -1, which simplifies and optimizes the code.
The commit removes a call to 'dtim_assoc' IOVAR because the firmware no longer supports it. This prevents error messages when roaming between access points.
The commit adds support for a platform without an amplifier by introducing a new board configuration (adl_rt5682) that connects the alc5682 codec to SSP0, following Intel's BT offload design.

The commit modifies how zerocopy sendmsg operations are handled when the socket does not support them. It ensures that such operations fail appropriately, similar to how previous patches handled zerocopy send requests.

The commit modifies the kernel documentation preamble to test for the existence of xeCJK.sty before attempting to load it. This change addresses an issue where certain distributions might encounter errors due to missing dependencies or incorrect package installations.
The commit modifies the checkpatch script to stop flagging certain 'case' macros as errors, allowing their use without triggering warnings.
The commit replaces simple-KMS helpers with atomic helpers to improve code architecture and facilitate easier code sharing among drivers. There are no functional changes.
The commit adds a JPEG decoding worker interface to ensure that three hardware components run in parallel on the MT8195 platform, enhancing media processing capabilities.

The commit removes unnecessary VPRINTK() calls and the ata_scsi_dump_cdb() function in the libata subsystem because their information is already covered by tracepoints. This improves code quality by eliminating redundant logging.

The commit adds support for entering zstate during extended vblank periods in FREESYNC_STATE_VIDEO mode. It calculates extra vblank time and allows zstate entry if the time exceeds 5ms, optimizing min_dst_y_next_start and restoring defaults upon exiting freesync.
The commit fixes a type mismatch in the hqd_destroy() callback function to resolve a CFI failure when running on specific hardware with Clang's kernel Control Flow Integrity enabled.
The commit corrects the property name from 'reset' to 'resets' in device tree bindings for STM32 pinctrl and adds validation to prevent future issues.
The commit addresses an array-bounds warning in GCC 12 by suppressing it, indicating potential issues with array access but not directly fixing them.

The commit replaces pm_runtime_get_sync with pm_runtime_resume_and_get in the sun8i-ss crypto driver. This change simplifies the code by using a more appropriate function for power management operations.

The commit reorganizes code related to fixed file installation and removal in the io_uring subsystem by moving it into the filetable section. It also modifies helper functions to use context (ctx) instead of request structures, improving code structure and maintainability.
The commit updates device tree bindings for media components on NXP i.MX8MQ VPU by splitting G1 and G2 decoder nodes into separate entities with proper power domain support.
The commit removes an unused macro (IWL_DEVICE_BZ_COMMON) within another macro (IWL_DEVICE_BZ), simplifying the configuration by combining their contents.
